mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lifts equity prices by lowering the discount rate and easing financial conditions. When the policy rate is falling, the S&P 500 should deliver positive 20-day returns; the effect is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with above-baseline forward performance.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with declining FEDFUNDS, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in CPIAUCSL above recent trend raises the chance of tighter policy and higher real rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3.0% while FEDFUNDS is not falling
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if the sign becomes positive in more than 40% of triggered cases over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should reduce near-term corporate revenue expectations and push S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal’s Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A broad retail-sales slowdown in RSAFS indicates softening consumer demand and weaker earnings momentum, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day risk-repricing window when the slowdown is abrupt.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth turns negative after being positive in the prior 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if abrupt RSAFS slowdowns fail to produce negative 5-day SPX returns on average, or if the triggered hit rate is not worse than 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and HOUST both strengthen, the economy is in a synchronized real-activity upswing that supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if industrial and housing activity do not move together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in INDPRO and HOUST do not outperform baseline on 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A recovery in GDPC1 after prior weakness signals improving aggregate demand and better earnings expectations, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is stronger when GDP growth is not being offset by labor deterioration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth turns positive after at least one quarter of slowdown
failure_condition: Reject if GDP recovery triggers do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS print indicates labor-market softening and weaker wage-income support for consumption, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal is clearest when employment losses persist for multiple months.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month employment declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses explanatory power after accounting for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: High FEDFUNDS combined with elevated CPIAUCSL creates a real-rate squeeze that tightens liquidity and compresses equity multiples. Under this regime, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next month, and the effect should fail when inflation is falling or policy is easing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation above 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are not worse than baseline by at least 2 percentage points in walk-forward testing.
